title: lupinus excubitus subsp. medius
intro: Lupinus excubitus subsp. medius, commonly known as the Channel Island bush lupine, is a subspecies of the lupine plant native to the Channel Islands off the coast of California. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that may aid in reducing pain and inflammation. The health benefits of Lupinus excubitus subsp. medius include potential uses in treating cardiovascular disease, diabetes, and certain types of cancer, although more research is needed to confirm these claims. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and infusions, can be made by steeping the dried flowers and leaves in hot water, although it is essential to consult with a medical professional before using any herbal remedy.
